title: Threat Alert Card
description: Output schema for the Sentinel agent. Defines the structure of a classified threat event.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the alert was generated

event_summary.description: Brief factual description of the event and its supply chain relevance

affected_regions.description: Geographic regions affected by the event

affected_routes.description: Shipping route IDs (RTE-XXXX) that may be disrupted

affected_commodities.description: Types of goods or commodities affected

source_articles.description: News articles that support this alert

confidence.description: HIGH: 5+ corroborating sources. MEDIUM: 3-4 sources. LOW: 1-2 sources.

data_freshness.description: How recent the source data is (e.g., '15 minutes', '1 hour', '24 hours')

event_subcategory.description: Specific subcategory within the event type (e.g., MILITARY_CONFLICT > Shipping Route Closure, NATURAL_DISASTER > Earthquake). Used to match the correct playbook template.

source_count.description: Number of distinct news sources corroborating this event. Used for confidence calibration: 5+ = HIGH, 3-4 = MEDIUM, 1-2 = LOW.

affected_countries.description: Specific country names affected by the event. Atlas uses this to match against supplier.country field directly.

estimated_duration.description: Estimated duration of the disruption (e.g., '7-14 days', '30-90 days', 'Indefinite'). Based on historical patterns for this crisis type. Critical for inventory planning.

affected_sectors.description: Industry sectors impacted by this event. Helps Atlas filter suppliers by sector before geographic filtering.
